# Figure Plan — YOLO11m + CBAM + P2 (defense report)

Single source of truth for every figure: what it is, whether the code really
produces it, how to make a quick Gemini draft, and the exact draw.io / script to
run. Two kinds of figures:

- **Schematic** — I draw them (draw.io); Gemini can make a preliminary raster.
- **Real output** — generated by running your code on the GPU PC against `best.pt`
  and a C2A test image. Gemini **cannot** fabricate these (they are real model
  activations / detections) — you must run the script.

---

---

## 2. Order of operations (do it in this sequence)

1. **GPU PC — run `make_arch_figure_images.py`** → #10–13 (input, detections, CBAM overlay, P2 map). Fastest win, unblocks Chapter III explainability. *(~2 min)*
2. **GPU PC — run the detection-grid script** (§3.2) → #14, the real-data companion to the stride figure.
3. **GPU PC — re-run SAHI + TTA on CBAM+P2 `best.pt`** (§3.3) → refreshed numbers **and** input/output crops for the #15/#16 paste-slots. This is the one open experimental item (FINDINGS §3).
4. **This PC — finish the 2 schematic draw.io** (#8 waterfall, #9 CBAM effect), then **export all `.drawio` → PNG/PDF**.
5. **Paste all PNGs into `figures/`, compile.** Update Chapter IV numbers only after step 3.

> Steps 1–3 need the GPU PC (ultralytics + `best.pt`). Steps 4–5 are on this machine.

---

## 3. Per-figure treatment

### 3.1 Real explainability set — CBAM overlay, P2 feature map, input, detections (#10–13)

**i. What I want.** Four 640×640 PNGs from the recommended CBAM+P2 model on one C2A
test image: the raw input, the predicted boxes, the CBAM spatial-attention heat-map,
and the P2 (stride-4) feature response. They make the architecture chapter concrete
("this is what the model actually sees / attends to").

**ii. What the code does — does it really do it? ✅ Verified.**
`Last Month/24_01_26- Benchmarking YOLOs/CBAM_P2Head/make_arch_figure_images.py`:
- `arch_input.png` — reads the test image, resizes to 640. *(line 132–134)*
- `arch_detections.png` — `yolo.predict(conf=0.25)`, plots thin boxes, no labels. *(line 137–140)*
- `cbam_overlay.png` — forward hook on `layers[10].spatial_attention.sigmoid`, JET heat-map at α=0.5. *(line 147–155)* — **this is real spatial attention.**
- `p2_featuremap.png` — forward hook on `layers[19]` (the P2 C3k2 output), mean over channels → 160×160 → VIRIDIS. *(line 149–161)* — **real stride-4 feature.**
- Auto-finds newest `runs/**/weights/best.pt` and the image `collapsed_building_image0001_3.png`. CBAM classes are defined verbatim so `YOLO(best.pt)` unpickles. Runs directly.

**Caveats to know:** CBAM sits at **P5 (stride 32)**, so `cbam_overlay` is a coarse 20×20 map upscaled to 640 — it will look blocky; that is correct, not a bug. `p2_featuremap` is fine (160×160). One `cbam_spatial_overlay.png` already exists in `figures/` — check whether it's from this exact model before reusing.

**iii. Gemini prompt.** N/A — these are real model outputs; a generated image would be fiction. (If you only want a grey placeholder box for layout while the run is pending, say so and I'll add one to the draw.io.)

**iv. How to run.**
```bash
# on the GPU PC, in the CBAM_P2Head folder (the one containing runs/.../best.pt):
python make_arch_figure_images.py
# then copy arch_fig_out/*.png  ->  Defense/draft1_30_6_26/figures/
```
Optional: set `CROP = (x1,y1,x2,y2)` in the script to zoom into a people cluster if the tiny targets read too small.

---

### 3.2 Detection grid on a real C2A test image (#14)

**i. What I want.** A real C2A test crop with the model's detections **and** a stride
grid drawn over it, so the reader sees actual tiny humans spanning only a few cells —
the real-data companion to the schematic `fig_stride_problem`.

**ii. What the code does — does it really do it? ❌ Not yet.**
`make_arch_figure_images.py` gives input + detections but **draws no grid**. No existing
script overlays detection-grid cells. → new script below (drop it in the same folder).

**iii. Gemini prompt.** N/A — real image.

**iv. Script to run** (save as `make_detgrid_image.py` next to `best.pt`):
```python
import glob, numpy as np, cv2, torch, torch.nn as nn
from pathlib import Path
# --- paste the ChannelAttention / SpatialAttention / CBAM / register_cbam() block
#     from make_arch_figure_images.py here (verbatim) so best.pt unpickles ---
from ultralytics import YOLO

SCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
IMG_NAME = "collapsed_building_image0001_3.png"
IMGSZ, CONF = 640, 0.25
STRIDE = 8            # grid cell size in px: 32=P5, 16=P4, 8=P3, 4=P2
CROP = (180,120,520,470)  # zoom to a people cluster in 640-space; set None for full frame

W = Path(max(glob.glob(str(SCRIPT_DIR/"runs"/"**"/"weights"/"best.pt"), recursive=True),
             key=lambda p: Path(p).stat().st_mtime))
IMG = Path(glob.glob(str(next(b for b in [SCRIPT_DIR,*SCRIPT_DIR.parents]
        if glob.glob(str(b/"**"/"test"/"images"/IMG_NAME), recursive=True))
        /"**"/"test"/"images"/IMG_NAME), recursive=True)[0])
yolo = YOLO(str(W))
res = yolo.predict(str(IMG), imgsz=IMGSZ, conf=CONF, verbose=False)
img = cv2.resize(res[0].plot(line_width=1, labels=False, conf=False), (IMGSZ, IMGSZ))
for x in range(0, IMGSZ, STRIDE):           # draw the grid
    cv2.line(img, (x,0), (x,IMGSZ), (200,200,200), 1)
    cv2.line(img, (0,x), (IMGSZ,x), (200,200,200), 1)
if CROP:
    x1,y1,x2,y2 = CROP; img = img[y1:y2, x1:x2]
out = SCRIPT_DIR/"arch_fig_out"/f"detgrid_c2a_s{STRIDE}.png"; out.parent.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
cv2.imwrite(str(out), img); print("wrote", out)
```
Run once at `STRIDE=8` (P3) and once at `STRIDE=4` (P2) to show the coarse-vs-fine contrast on the same crop.

### 3.3 SAHI & TTA (#15, #16)

**i. What I want.** The two pipeline diagrams (done) with their PASTE-slots filled by a
real full-image input and a merged-detections output, plus **numbers refreshed for the
CBAM+P2 model** (Chapter IV).

**ii. What the code does — does it really do it? ⚠️ Partly / stale.**
The SAHI/TTA runs in the FINDINGS doc were done in March on the **old Mamba+CBAM+P2**
model (FINDINGS §3), and those scripts are **not in the current `Last Month/` tree**.
Per your 2026-07-02 decision the CBAM+P2 re-run is **pending** — so the diagram config
is real, but there are no CBAM+P2 numbers or crops yet. Minimal runnable snippets below.

**iii. Gemini prompt.** The diagrams already exist; Gemini is optional. If you want a
raster of the pipeline, reuse the style header from `GEMINI_c3k2mamba_prompt.md` and
describe the slice-grid → detector → GREEDYNMM-merge flow.

**iv. draw.io (done) + scripts to run.**
Diagrams: `fig_sahi.drawio`, `fig_tta.drawio` (already grounded in the config below).

SAHI (needs the CBAM register block first, then):
```python
from sahi import AutoDetectionModel
from sahi.predict import get_sliced_prediction
m = AutoDetectionModel.from_pretrained(model_type="ultralytics",
        model_path="runs/.../weights/best.pt", confidence_threshold=0.15, device="cuda")
r = get_sliced_prediction(str(IMG), m,
        slice_height=512, slice_width=512,
        overlap_height_ratio=0.25, overlap_width_ratio=0.25,
        perform_standard_pred=True,
        postprocess_type="GREEDYNMM", postprocess_match_metric="IOS",
        postprocess_match_threshold=0.5)
r.export_visuals(export_dir="sahi_out")     # -> the merged-detections crop for the slot
```
TTA (test-time augmentation is just `augment=True`):
```python
from ultralytics import YOLO
m = YOLO("runs/.../weights/best.pt")
m.val(data="data.yaml", split="test", imgsz=1280, augment=True)   # refreshed TTA numbers
m.predict(str(IMG), imgsz=1280, augment=True, save=True)          # crop for the slot
```
Sweep for the report: SAHI slice 256/320/512/640, overlap 0.25–0.30, conf 0.15–0.20; TTA imgsz 1280 (1920 degrades). Then replace the old-Mamba numbers in Chapter IV.

### 3.4 Ablation waterfall (#8) — schematic, to build

**i. What I want.** A waterfall on AP50: baseline → +CBAM → +P2 → +Mamba, with a
params·latency line under each column, so one figure carries the whole finding —
including the honest Mamba null at 2.8× latency.

**ii. Numbers — verified (FINDINGS §1).**
| step | AP50 | Δ | params (M) | latency e2e (ms) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Baseline | 0.8432 | — | 20.03 | 13.68 |
| +CBAM | 0.8473 | +0.0041 | 19.08 | 13.48 |
| +CBAM+P2 (recommended) | 0.8533 | +0.0060 | 19.57 | 14.55 |
| +Mamba (negative) | 0.8521 | −0.0012 | 22.01 | **41.06** |
Framing: state Mamba as "no meaningful accuracy change (±noise: −0.0012 AP50, +0.0014 AR_100) at 2.8× latency". Zoom the y-axis to 0.838–0.856 and label it zoomed.

**iii. Gemini prompt.** "Waterfall bar chart, 4 columns Baseline/+CBAM/+CBAM+P2/+Mamba, y-axis AP50 zoomed 0.838–0.856; green floating bars for gains (+0.0041, +0.0060), a red downward bar for −0.0012; dashed step connectors; under each column its params and latency; +CBAM+P2 outlined in red as recommended, +Mamba latency 41 ms in red. Serif font, white background, flat vector."

**iv. draw.io.** Design locked; I'll generate `fig_ablation_waterfall.drawio` on your go.

### 3.5 CBAM effect (#9) — schematic, to build

**i. What I want.** Before→after feature tiles showing clutter (flood/rubble) down-weighted
and the faint human amplified — the *effect* of attention, not its wiring. Pairs with the
real `cbam_overlay.png`.

**ii. Grounded in.** The mechanism is exactly what §3.1's hook visualizes; the schematic
is illustrative and should be captioned "illustrative", with the real overlay cited beside it.

**iii. Gemini prompt.** "Three elements left to right: a feature-map tile with several
orange background blobs and one faint grey blob (label 'before CBAM, illustrative'); a
green box 'CBAM: channel then spatial attention'; a feature-map tile where the background
blobs are faded and the central blob is bright red (label 'after CBAM'). Caption: clutter
suppressed, target amplified. Serif font, white background, flat vector."

**iv. draw.io.** I'll generate `fig_cbam_effect.drawio` on your go.

## 4. Caption numbers cheat-sheet (keep captions airtight)

- **Ablation (COCO test, AdamW, 1 seed):** AP50 0.843 → 0.847 → **0.853** → 0.852; AR_100 0.691 → 0.692 → **0.703** → 0.704; latency 13.7 → 13.5 → 14.6 → **41.1 ms**; params 20.0 / 19.1 / 19.6 / 22.0 M. *(FINDINGS §1)*
- **Per-size recall (P2 model):** very-tiny 0.757 / tiny 0.870 / small 0.887 / medium 0.811. *(FINDINGS §1)*
- **C2A sizes:** 47 % < 10 px, 52 % 10–50 px, ~1 % > 50 px. *(Nihal et al., FINDINGS §5)*
- **Strides at 640:** P2 = 4, P3 = 8, P4 = 16, P5 = 32 px.
- **SAHI/TTA (old Mamba ref, re-run pending):** SAHI-512 F1 0.866 / VT-recall 0.808; SAHI-256 VT-recall 0.829; TTA@1280 mAP50 +1.64, miss-rate 14→10 %. *(FINDINGS §3)*

> Rule: dataset numbers cite Nihal et al.; all model numbers cite your run's `summary.json`. Never mix the two in one caption without saying which is which.
